data_in.description: MIST-CF repository (samgoldman97/mist-cf) containing sinusoidal formula embedding implementation
Representative set of chemical formulas for embedding verification (e.g., C6H12O6, C3H7NO2, C8H10N4O2)

data_out.description: Embedding vectors for each input chemical formula with shape (num_formulas, embedding_dimension)
Validation report confirming dimensionality, value range, and reproducibility against reference outputs

domain_knowledge: Sinusoidal embeddings encode positional or categorical information using sine and cosine basis functions to produce continuous vector representations without learnable parameters.
Chemical formula embedding dimensionality must be consistent across the model pipeline; typical dimensions range from 64 to 512 for transformer-based architectures.
Sinusoidal embeddings are deterministic and reproducible; identical input formulas must always produce identical output vectors under the same configuration.
Value range for sinusoidal embeddings typically spans [-1, 1] due to the bounded nature of trigonometric functions.

direct_checks: Verify file exists: the MIST-CF repository (samgoldman97/mist-cf) contains a sinusoidal formula embedding implementation or module
Verify script runs: a computational agent can load the embedding layer from the MIST-CF codebase and execute it on a test set of chemical formulas without errors
Verify output_matches_reference: embedding vectors produced by the agent's implementation have the same dimensionality as reported in SCARF or MIST-CF documentation
Verify value_in_range: all numerical values in the output embedding vectors fall within a defensible range (e.g. [-1, 1] for sinusoidal embeddings), robust to parameter choices
Verify row_count_equals: the number of output embedding vectors equals the number of input chemical formulas
expert_review: Confirm that the sinusoidal embedding formula and hyperparameters (e.g., frequency scaling, periodicity, dimension) match the SCARF paper specification and are correctly transcribed into the implementation
Assess reproducibility: expert inspection of whether computed embeddings exhibit expected periodicity and symmetry properties characteristic of sinusoidal encodings

Prepare a representative set of chemical formulas for embedding.
Compute embedding vectors by invoking the embedding layer forward pass on each formula.
Verify embedding dimensionality matches the configured parameter.

Validation: embeddings match reference outputs with L2 distance < 1e-6 and all values are in [-1, 1].

task_description: Implement the sinusoidal formula embedding layer as used in MIST-CF and verify that embedding vectors produced for a set of chemical formulas match expected numerical properties (dimensionality, value range, reproducibility) against reference outputs from the MIST-CF repository.

task_inputs.label: Set of chemical formulas to embed

task_objective: Implement sinusoidal formula embeddings for chemical formulas and validate embedding vectors produce correct dimensionality, value ranges, and reproducible outputs.

task_outputs.label: Sinusoidal embedding vectors for chemical formulas
Numerical property validation and reproducibility check results

title: Reconstruct sinusoidal formula embeddings from the SCARF component within MIST-CF
uncertainty_notes: This card was generated by the LLM-assisted pipeline and needs scientific expert review.
Each TracedClaim's evidence_span has been substring-checked against its source section; see logs/llm_calls.jsonl and capsules/<task_id>/quality_report.json for groundedness results.
workflow_description: 1. Clone the MIST-CF repository (samgoldman97/mist-cf) and locate the sinusoidal formula embedding implementation adopted from SCARF. 2. Load or define a representative set of chemical formulas (e.g., common organic molecules: glucose C6H12O6, alanine C3H7NO2, caffeine C8H10N4O2). 3. Instantiate the sinusoidal embedding layer with its configured dimensionality and invoke the forward pass on each formula to produce embedding vectors. 4. Verify embedding vector dimensionality matches the configured embedding dimension parameter. 5. Verify all embedding values fall within the expected numerical range (typically [-1, 1] for sinusoidal embeddings). 6. Compare produced embeddings against reference outputs from the MIST-CF repository using numerical tolerance (e.g., L2 distance < 1e-6) to confirm reproducibility. 7. Output embedding vectors and validation results to a structured file.
